Transcrição Completa: 27 What Model Is Better Transactional Or Recurring

 

[00:00] recurring revenue or transactional revenue. Do you want to get paid every month or do you want to get paid one time and then have to go out and get new clients every time?

 

[00:09] To me, it comes down to how many yeses you want to get and continue getting in your agency.

 

[00:14] So, let's look at this over an entire year, okay? Let's take ourselves outside of our day-to-day perspective and look at this over a 12-month period of time.

 

[00:23] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Modelo Recorrente - Cenário Anual

 

NÚMEROS: 4 clientes × $25K/mês = $100K/mês × 12 meses = $1.2M/ano

 

VISUAL: Comparação visual entre modelo recorrente e transacional, destaque para receita previsível mensal, representação de 4 clientes gerando $100K mensais

 

[00:25] If I told you that if four people paid you 25K each, you'd have $100,000 predictably each month. and that you could maintain great relationships with less clients and put more of your attention on them and more results towards them.

 

[00:41] Well, $100,000 a month times 12 would get you to $1.2 million here in the US over a year. So $1.2 million on four clients.

 

[00:50] That's not a lot of time and effort most likely if you're doing it on a result-driven service you're selling.

 

[00:56] And long story short, it's predictable because it's recurring revenue. And because you can say yourself confidently, you know, my team and I, we can focus on just these four people and they're worth enough to us to where we have additional rewards that can come if we get them even more of a result.

 

[01:13] And we love this specific model. It's predictably 1.2 million a year.

 

[01:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Receita Adicional - Comissões de Referência

 

NÚMEROS: $1.2M base + $20K/mês em kickbacks = $240K adicional/ano

 

VISUAL: Modelo de receita dupla: receita recorrente principal + comissões de referência, setas mostrando fluxo de referências para outras agências

 

[01:20] We then take some of those profits that come in. I reinvest them into services that are a little less time intensive or just easy to outsource or I as deals come to me that I'm not going to take on in my own agency refer them to other people and I get a kickback on that.

 

[01:36] Therefore, although I have four clients and $1.2 million a year predictably from my $100,000 a month times 12, I might also get an additional $20,000 a month in kickback commissions from me and my agency referring deals to other people that we don't do ourselves.

 

[01:52] That's a very healthy, sustainable, predictable model.

 

[01:56] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Modelo Transacional - A Armadilha

 

NÚMEROS: 50 clientes × $1K = $50K/mês (metade do modelo recorrente)

 

VISUAL: Comparação visual desfavorável: 50 clientes vs 4 clientes, destaque negativo para volume necessário, representação de esforço multiplicado

 

[01:58] What if I told you the person, and there are people like this out there, that want to sell transactionally? So, they think, "Okay, there's no way somebody's going to pay me $10 to $25,000 a month. I can probably get more people to give me $1,000."

 

[02:14] And this is how they think, dude. You see, I get a little more spread that I can probably get at least 50 people a month to pay me $1,000. And that's $50,000 a month. And I just need 50 people to give me that.

 

[02:26] Okay. Well, that person is probably thinking short term. It's a reactive person who's not thinking in the long term because think about this.

 

[02:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Comparação de Esforço - Yeses Necessários

 

NÚMEROS: Modelo Recorrente: 4 yeses | Modelo Transacional: 50 yeses/mês

 

VISUAL: Gráfico comparativo dramático entre 4 vs 50 aprovações necessárias, destaque visual para diferença de esforço, contraste entre simplicidade e complexidade

 

[02:37] The person over here, they have to get four yeses. They have to get four people to say yes to achieving that business outcome.

 

[02:44] Whereas over here, the person who wants to make $50,000 a month, which is less money than $100,000 a month, wants 50 clients at $1,000 a pop.

 

[02:54] Maybe in your head, you think you're smart and you're saying, "Whatever, Jeremy. I'll get 25 clients at $2,000 a pop or $2,500 a pop. It doesn't matter if you're thinking that level because you're going to get burned out on your time and on the effort that's required and you're going to have to get more people to say yes, which mathematically requires more effort and more time.

 

[03:17] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Matemática do Modelo Transacional

 

NÚMEROS: 50 clientes/mês × 10% taxa de fechamento = 500 leads necessários/mês × $10/lead = $5K em custo de aquisição

 

VISUAL: Cálculo detalhado mostrando funil de vendas: 500 leads → 50 clientes, representação de custos de aquisição, gráfico de conversão 10%

 

[03:19] See, every month, if I'm selling a transactional service and I only need 50 people at $1,000 a pop, let's do the math. That means I have to get 50 people per month to say yes, I want to sign up for your service.

 

[03:31] That means each month I have to produce the amount of leads. Let's say I have a 10% closing ratio to get 50 people a month to say yes. That means I'm going to need about 500 leads.

 

[03:42] So I'm going to need to spend or put in the amount of effort that's required to generate 500 leads. Which if you're spending money to do that, let's say you average out at $10 a lead. Okay?

 

[03:52] So you got 500 time 10. You're going to spend $5,000 to generate your 500 leads.

 

[03:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Modelo Recorrente - Eficiência de Aquisição

 

NÚMEROS: 4-20 perfect cold video pitches = 4 clientes (taxa de conversão muito superior)

 

VISUAL: Contraste visual: 20 pitches de vídeo vs 500 leads, destaque para eficiência do método, representação de taxa de fechamento elevada

 

[04:00] Whereas this person over here, maybe they just send four perfect cold video pitches or 20 perfect cold video pitches to land the four clients because the perfect cold video pitch will have a higher close closing ratio anyway.

 

[04:13] Over here, you're going to have to put out what about eight times more effort just to get less money with more time and effort involved.

 

[04:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Análise Anual - Escala do Pesadelo

 

NÚMEROS: Modelo Transacional: 500 leads/mês × 12 meses = 6,000 leads/ano | Modelo Recorrente: esforço de 1 mês para 12 meses de receita

 

VISUAL: Gráfico de escala anual dramático, multiplicação 12x do esforço mensal no modelo transacional vs esforço único no recorrente, representação visual de burnout

 

[04:24] And on top of that, when the next month comes around, so once again, if we're looking at this over 12 months, you have to produce that effort 12x.

 

[04:33] Whereas this person over here who has recurring revenue, they had to put that effort out for maybe a month to retain those year-long relationships with four people that said yes.

 

[04:42] Over here, you're going to have to have 500 leads a month. So 500 times 12, that's going to be how many leads you need per year.

 

[04:49] That means the amount of time and effort that you put in one month multiplied times 12. That means the amount of closing time, the amount of time that you spend working with each client just to get them results.

 

[05:00] The amount of times you're going to have to literally swipe a credit card. You have to get 12 times as many yeses over a year with 8x the additional effort per month comparatively to the guy over here.

 

[05:14] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Conclusão - Modelo Recorrente Vence

 

NÚMEROS: 8x menos esforço, 12x menos yeses necessários, 2x mais receita ($100K vs $50K/mês)

 

VISUAL: Quadro comparativo final definitivo: menos clientes + preços mais altos = menos esforço + mais lucro + relacionamentos melhores, destaque para superioridade do modelo recorrente

 

[05:16] So my point is, you want to make sure you understand this very loud and clear. Okay? Transactional revenue is a nightmare. One-time revenue is a failure point in your agency.

 

[05:27] It is a way you can almost assure yourself that you're going to fuck over yourself and your team on the time and effort that's involved to get paid less simply because you don't you you can't make the decision to instead charge high ticket and for recurring revenue.

 

[05:44] less people, higher costs per client, requires less time and effort, less yeses, and will likely get you paid more if you price the packages right, and you focus on developing and maintaining those relationship with those clients that you close.

 

[06:00] Even if you lose one client or two clients throughout the year, still going to take less effort to replace those clients than it is over here, where you're going to have a lot more time, energy, and attention put in per deal cycle.

 

[06:13] All right, hope this helps. to see you in the next video.

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

Modelo Recorrente: 4 clientes × $25K/mês = $100K/mês = $1.2M/ano

Receita adicional: +$20K/mês em comissões de referência = +$240K/ano

Modelo Transacional: 50 clientes × $1K = $50K/mês (metade da receita)

Taxa de fechamento: 10% no modelo transacional

Leads necessários: 500/mês no transacional vs 4-20 pitches no recorrente

Custo de aquisição: $10/lead × 500 = $5K/mês em marketing

Esforço anual: 6,000 leads/ano no transacional vs esforço de 1 mês no recorrente

Multiplicador de esforço: 8x mais trabalho no transacional para metade da receita

Yeses necessários: 50/mês × 12 = 600/ano no transacional vs 4 no recorrente

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Modelo de receita recorrente com 4 clientes high-ticket

Perfect cold video pitch como método de aquisição de alta conversão

Sistema de comissões de referência para deals não atendidos

Reinvestimento de lucros em serviços de baixa intensidade ou terceirizados

Foco em relacionamentos de longo prazo (contratos anuais)

Manutenção de poucos clientes com alta atenção e resultados

Reposição eficiente de clientes perdidos (1-2 por ano)

 

ESTRUTURA:

Framework de comparação: Recorrente vs Transacional

Análise de 12 meses para perspectiva de longo prazo

Cálculo de esforço: leads necessários × taxa de conversão × meses

Estrutura de preços: High-ticket recorrente ($25K/mês) vs Low-ticket transacional ($1K único)

Modelo de dupla receita: base recorrente + comissões de referência

Sistema de relacionamento cliente-agência de alta qualidade

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS: Modelo Recorrente:

Receita mensal: $100,000

Receita anual: $1,200,000

Número de clientes: 4

Ticket médio: $25,000/mês

Receita adicional (referências): $20,000/mês = $240,000/ano

Receita total potencial: $1,440,000/ano

Custo de aquisição: Mínimo (4-20 video pitches)

 

Modelo Transacional:

Receita mensal: $50,000

Receita anual: $600,000

Número de clientes: 50/mês = 600/ano

Ticket médio: $1,000 (pagamento único)

Leads necessários: 500/mês = 6,000/ano

Custo de aquisição: $5,000/mês = $60,000/ano

Taxa de conversão: 10%

Esforço: 8x maior que modelo recorrente

Yeses necessários: 12x mais que modelo recorrente

 

Comparação ROI:

Modelo Recorrente: 2x mais receita com 8x menos esforço

Modelo Transacional: Metade da receita com 12x mais trabalho

Custo de oportunidade: $600K/ano de diferença em receita

Eficiência de tempo: Recorrente permite foco em qualidade vs quantidade

 

DURAÇÃO: ~6:15 minutos FORMATO: Apresentação educacional com análise comparativa matemática
